raw_consumer_statement:
XXXX I booked a " XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX' at XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, from XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX ( XXXX nights ) through the Citibank travel website, and paid a total of {$1200.00} ( at CITI XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX DE made on XX/XX/XXXX2024 ). 

Before XX/XX/XXXX I confirmed the booking details with the hotel, and the hotel informed me that they had received the reservation, but my reservation was for a " XXXX XXXX, '' not the " XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX I had communicated through the Citibank travel website.

XXXX Upon arrival at the hotel, the front desk confirmed that the reservation they received was indeed for a " XXXX XXXX '' They advised me to contact Citibank XXXX  directly. I called Citibank customer service three times without success : twice the call was dropped after 30 minutes of ringing, and once the call was cut off midway. To stay in the room I originally booked, I was told I would have to pay an additional XXXX yen ( around USD XXXX ) per night to upgrade. 

XX/XX/XXXX I filed a dispute with Citibank, requesting a refund of the difference ( {$55.00} x XXXX ), totaling USD XXXX, for the forced downgrade. 

XXXX Citibanks dispute center rejected my claim, stating that there were no contractual errors. 

XX/XX/XXXX I filed another appeal, and Citibank responded, acknowledging receipt of my supporting documents. 

XX/XX/XXXX Citibanks dispute center rejected my dispute again.
